description: Round 3 hardening: unbounded queries, missing request cancellation, cache invalidation gaps, focus trap accessibility, prop drilling reduction, and response envelope consistency.

userStories.title: Add pagination to _get_applied_urls in jobs/service.py
userStories.description: backend/jobs/service.py line 54: _get_applied_urls() uses .to_list(length=None) which loads ALL applied URLs into memory. For users with thousands of applications this causes memory spikes. Change to .to_list(length=5000) with a projection that only returns source_url field (not full documents). Also add a structlog warning if the result count equals the limit, indicating potential truncation.
acceptanceCriteria: .to_list() uses an explicit length limit instead of None
Query uses projection to only return source_url field
Warning logged if result count equals limit
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Memory safety — unbounded .to_list(length=None) is a time bomb for power users.

userStories.title: Add KEYS.stats invalidation to useBulkEditApplications mutation
userStories.description: frontend/src/hooks/useApplications.js lines 218-226: useBulkEditApplications() only invalidates KEYS.all on success but does NOT invalidate KEYS.stats. Compare to useBulkUpdateApplicationStage() at lines 154-163 which correctly invalidates both. Add queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: KEYS.stats }) to the onSuccess callback.
acceptanceCriteria: useBulkEditApplications onSuccess invalidates both KEYS.all and KEYS.stats
Dashboard stats update correctly after bulk tag/field edits
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Data consistency — stats show stale counts after bulk edits until manual refresh.

userStories.title: Add focus trap to DetailPanel modal for keyboard accessibility
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/DetailPanel.jsx line 59 has aria-modal='true' but no focus trap. Keyboard users can tab outside the panel to the list behind it, violating WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.3. Add a useEffect that: (1) saves the previously focused element on mount, (2) focuses the first focusable element in the panel, (3) traps Tab/Shift+Tab within the panel, (4) restores focus to the saved element on unmount.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab key cycles through focusable elements within DetailPanel only
Shift+Tab cycles backwards within DetailPanel
Focus is set to first focusable element when panel opens
Focus returns to the previously focused element when panel closes
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Accessibility — WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.3 compliance. aria-modal without focus trap is misleading.

userStories.title: Reduce DetailPanel prop drilling by grouping handler props into single object
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/DetailPanel.jsx line 12: PanelContent receives 11 individual props including handleDelete, handleStageChange, handleUpdate, handleUndoDelete, handleUndoDismiss, onAddEvent, setNotesDirty. Group the action handlers into a single 'actions' object prop: { onDelete, onStageChange, onUpdate, onUndoDelete, onUndoDismiss, onAddEvent, onDirtyChange }. Update PanelContent, DetailPanelHeader, and PanelBody to destructure from actions.
acceptanceCriteria: PanelContent receives an 'actions' object prop instead of 7+ individual handler props
Total prop count on PanelContent reduced to 5 or fewer
All child components updated to use actions.onXxx pattern
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Code simplicity — 11 props is hard to maintain and reason about.

userStories.title: Add AbortController to useApplications query for request cancellation
userStories.description: frontend/src/hooks/useApplications.js: the useApplications() hook's queryFn calls fetchApplications() without passing an AbortSignal. React Query provides a signal via the queryFn context parameter. Pass this signal to the axios request so queries are cancelled when component unmounts or filters change. Update fetchApplications in api/applications.js to accept an optional signal parameter and pass it to axios via { signal }.
acceptanceCriteria: useApplications queryFn passes context.signal to fetchApplications
fetchApplications accepts and forwards signal to axios
Rapid filter changes cancel previous in-flight requests
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Performance & correctness — prevents stale data overwrites from old requests completing after new ones.

userStories.title: Standardize response envelope with meta on list endpoints
userStories.description: backend/saved_searches/router.py lines 89-92 returns {data: [...], meta: {total}} but other list endpoints like /api/applications, /api/contacts return {data: [...]} without meta. Add meta: {total} to the list_applications endpoint in applications/router.py and list_contacts endpoint in contacts/router.py. The total count should come from the existing count query or be derived from the result length when unpaginated.
acceptanceCriteria: list_applications returns {data: [...], meta: {total: N}}
list_contacts returns {data: [...], meta: {total: N}}
Existing frontend code still works (meta is additive, not breaking)
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: API consistency — standardize the envelope across all list endpoints per CLAUDE.md contract.

userStories.title: Move OPENAI_MODEL from hardcoded config to environment variable
userStories.description: backend/config.py line 52: openai_model is hardcoded as 'gpt-4o-mini'. This should be configurable via OPENAI_MODEL environment variable with 'gpt-4o-mini' as default. This allows switching models in production without code changes (e.g., for testing gpt-4o or future models).
acceptanceCriteria: openai_model reads from OPENAI_MODEL env var with 'gpt-4o-mini' default
All code that references settings.openai_model still works
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Configuration — models change frequently; hardcoding requires code deploys to switch.

userStories.title: Add test coverage for AI quota enforcement in ai_cache.py
userStories.description: backend/parsing/ai_cache.py has quota enforcement logic (check_and_increment_quota) but no dedicated test file. Create backend/tests/test_ai_cache.py with tests for: (1) quota increments correctly, (2) quota resets on new day, (3) quota exceeded returns appropriate error, (4) concurrent quota checks (if atomicity fix from US-186 is merged). Use real MongoDB per project testing rules.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/tests/test_ai_cache.py exists with 4+ test functions
Tests cover: increment, daily reset, exceeded, and edge cases
Tests use real MongoDB (not mocks) per CLAUDE.md rules
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Testing gap — critical billing/quota logic has zero test coverage.
